mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing investors to discount future earnings and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in the consumer price index below 1.5% annualized triggers expectations of aggressive central bank easing, which rapidly lifts equity valuations and yields positive returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Equity markets remain flat or decline when CPIAUCSL drops below the threshold, with a win rate below 45% over 5-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration and wage stagnation, which suppresses consumer spending power and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative rank IC over any 24-month rolling window or fails to predict returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, providing a persistent positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which boosts near-term earnings revisions and generates positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration in recovery regimes fails to produce positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 50%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shifts.
